widgets_values:
To use inpainting, upload your image into the "Load Image" above, right click -> Open in MaskEditor -> mask out what you need -> press save.
Then edit everything you need in the subgraph to the right (e.g. resolution at width/height, seed, steps, sampler, prompt, etc).

Default SDXL resolution is 1024, but since Lustify V8 (Apex) supports 1536 res, you make use of that. If that doesn't work out - switch back to 1024 (e.g. 896x1152, 832x1216, 1024x1024).

You can always open the subgraph to see what's inside (press the little icon on the top right corner of the subgraph) |
                                      |
                                      V

Besides the more common 1024 resolution, Lustify V8 (Apex) was also trained on 1536 resolution. If you decide to go into the 1536 territory - here are some examples you could use (set these values manually into the width_override / height_override).

1536 x 1536
1344 x 1664 / 1664 x 1344 
1280 x 1920 / 1920 x 1280
1152 x 2048 / 2048 x 1152
To use ControlNet, load your input image down below and unbypass the "ControlNet In First Pass" in the "Groups Bypasser".

You can skip 2a and 2b completely if the quality from highres pass is decent enough already, or you can pick either 2a or 2b. Which one - depends on you. Tiled Upscale is less resource hungry, but also MUCH worse than SeedVR.
90% of the time I prefer the result from 2b (SeedVR).

Once you've generated an image you like, you really should unbypass the Upscale Pass. Otherwise you're just holding back the full potential of the model (not just Lustify, but literally any diffusion model).
If your first pass was made with 1536 resolution, lower the upscale_by value to around 1.2 (it's down below in this subgraph). For 1024 resolution first pass it should be around 1.4-1.5 (if you use the 4x upscale model like the one I do).
If you don't have sam_vit_b and face_yolov8m models downloaded, open Manager -> Model Manager -> search for sam and bbox face models in the search bar, press install.
If needed - use either this, or manual inpainting of the face in the #7.

If you don't have much VRAM, you probably need to either stick with tiled upscale (or ignore 2a and 2b completely), or play around with optimizations like blocks_to_swap and tile_size. You could also try different versions of SeedVR:
3b fp8 requires ~3.3 Gb VRAM. 3b Q4 - even less.
7b Q4 requires ~4.5 Gb VRAM.
7b fp16 requires ~16 Gb VRAM.

I'd suggest you to avoid the "sharp" variant as in my tests it was quiet bad and not exactly "sharp".

There is a setting called "shorter_edge". It downscales your image so that the shorter edge of it is equal to the value you've set. "WHY TF DO WE DO THAT?" you'd ask. Well, SeedVR does it magic best when it upscales a smaller image. It's a very clever upscaler, unlike the more classic ESRGAN models.
BUT! The upscaler obviously wasn't trained on a ton of adult content. If you downscale too aggressively, anatomy like nipples or genitalia can lose their shape. If your "bits" are looking a bit messy, just bump the "shorter_edge" value up. You'll lose a little bit of that SeedVR upscale "magic," but the details will stay much cleaner.

the skin enhancing model can be downloaded there
https://openmodeldb.info/models/1x-ITF-SkinDiffDetail-Lite-v1

OR  

https://openmodeldb.info/users/brigadeiro

They have a different look, I suggest you trying them all.
Adjust blend_factor as needed.
Put the models into the folder with all your upscale models.

old, ugly,(face out of frame:1.1), black and white, (face out of frame:1.1), black and white,

the reason to add film grain there, before seedvr, is simple:
1) it helps to achieve a better skin texture
2) for some reason, seedvr works better with a slightly noised image
